questions.question: Why should an async composable return an `error` ref instead of throwing?
options: Throwing crashes the component's `setup()` with no way to catch it declaratively
Vue's reactivity system cannot track thrown errors
Throwing prevents TypeScript from inferring the return type
Errors thrown inside `onMounted` are silently swallowed by Vue

questions.question: What is the purpose of exposing a `retry` function from a data-fetching composable?
options: To debounce repeated calls to the same URL
To reset the component's reactive state entirely
To let users recover from transient failures without remounting the component

questions.question: Which Vue lifecycle hook is used as an error boundary to catch unexpected runtime errors in child components?
